data.plantuml:
@startuml
title Automated palletizing cell
left to right direction
component "Cell PLC" as PLC
component "Safety PLC" as SPLC
component "6-axis robot" as ROBOT
component "Robot controller" as RCTRL
component "Vacuum gripper" as TOOL
component "Infeed conveyor" as CONV
component "Pallet station" as PALLET
component "Safety laser scanner" as SCAN
component "Guard-door interlock" as GATE
component "Emergency-stop circuit" as ESTOP
component "Pneumatic supply" as AIR
component "Operator HMI" as HMI

PLC --> RCTRL : production commands
RCTRL --> ROBOT : servo motion
ROBOT --> TOOL : mechanical interface
PLC --> CONV : conveyor control
SPLC --> RCTRL : safe stop request
SCAN --> SPLC : protective field
GATE --> SPLC : access status
ESTOP --> SPLC : emergency stop
AIR --> TOOL : vacuum generation
HMI --> PLC : mode and recipe
@enduml

situations.name: Automatic palletizing
situations.description: Robot transfers sealed cartons from the infeed conveyor to a pallet while perimeter guarding is closed.

situations.name: Pallet exchange
situations.description: A forklift or pallet truck removes a completed pallet and places an empty pallet at the station.

situations.name: Conveyor jam recovery
situations.description: An operator accesses the infeed area to remove a skewed or damaged carton.

situations.name: Robot teaching
situations.description: A trained technician enters the safeguarded space and moves the robot using the teach pendant.

situations.name: Gripper maintenance
situations.description: Maintenance personnel inspect vacuum cups, hoses, valves, and retained pneumatic energy.

situations.name: Scanner muted for material flow
situations.description: The pallet opening permits controlled material transfer while muting or zoning logic distinguishes material from people.

situations.name: Power restoration
situations.description: Electrical power or compressed air returns after an interruption while personnel may remain near the cell.

situations.name: Software recipe change
situations.description: An engineer changes carton dimensions, pallet pattern, speeds, or robot positions for a new product.

hazards.name: Crushing by robot motion
hazards.description: A person is trapped between the moving robot, tooling, conveyor, pallet load, or fixed cell structure.

hazards.name: Impact from unexpected motion
hazards.description: Unexpected robot or conveyor movement strikes a person during production, setup, recovery, or restart.

hazards.name: Dropped carton or load
hazards.description: Loss of vacuum or poor gripping releases a carton onto a person or creates an unstable pallet.

hazards.name: Access through pallet opening
hazards.description: A person enters the safeguarded space through the pallet-transfer opening while hazardous motion remains possible.

hazards.name: Conveyor entanglement or pinch
hazards.description: Hands, clothing, or body parts are caught at conveyor rollers, transfers, guides, or moving cartons.

hazards.name: Stored pneumatic energy
hazards.description: Retained pressure causes unexpected gripper movement, hose motion, or release of a held carton during maintenance.

hazards.name: Electrical exposure
hazards.description: A person contacts hazardous voltage during troubleshooting, damaged wiring, or cabinet access.

hazards.name: Unstable palletized load
hazards.description: Incorrect placement or damaged cartons create an unstable stack that can collapse during exchange or transport.

requirements.text: Opening the interlocked access gate during automatic operation shall initiate a protective stop and prevent automatic restart until the gate is closed and a deliberate reset is performed outside the safeguarded space.
requirements.verification: Gate-interlock functional test VT-01, including restart prevention

requirements.text: The pallet-opening scanner function shall initiate a protective stop when a person enters the configured access field while allowing only validated pallet-transfer sequences.
requirements.verification: Protective-field and muting validation VT-02 using person and pallet test pieces

requirements.text: Following restoration of electrical power or compressed air, hazardous motion shall remain inhibited until safety inputs are valid and an operator performs a deliberate reset.
requirements.verification: Power and air interruption recovery test VT-03

requirements.text: The gripper shall monitor vacuum level during robot motion and initiate a controlled stop before the retained carton can leave the defined containment area when vacuum falls below the validated threshold.
requirements.verification: Vacuum-loss fault-injection test VT-04 across carton types and robot poses

requirements.text: Teach mode shall limit robot speed and require continuous enabling-device actuation for commanded motion inside the safeguarded space.
requirements.verification: Teach-mode speed measurement and enabling-device test VT-05

requirements.text: A recipe change affecting carton dimensions, pallet pattern, robot path, speed, payload, tooling, or protective fields shall require documented change-impact review before production release.
requirements.verification: Configuration-management audit and release checklist VT-06

requirements.text: Pneumatic energy at the gripper maintenance point shall be isolatable, lockable, and visibly vented before maintenance begins.
requirements.verification: Isolation inspection and zero-energy verification VT-07

silAssessments.hazardousEvent: An operator enters to clear a carton while the robot or conveyor can move.
silAssessments.safeState: Robot and conveyor stopped with restart inhibited
silAssessments.evidence: Gate and scanner validation plan VP-01

silAssessments.safetyFunction: Safe limited speed and enabling control during teaching
silAssessments.hazardousEvent: Robot moves faster than intended while a technician is inside the safeguarded space.
silAssessments.safeState: Motion stopped when enabling control is released or fully depressed

silAssessments.hazardousEvent: A person passes through the pallet opening while muting is active and hazardous motion continues.

rows.failureMode: Scanner output remains clear and is detected
rows.localEffect: Intrusion may be missed until diagnostic response
rows.diagnostic: Internal diagnostics and safety communication timeout

rows.failureMode: Scanner output remains clear and is not detected
rows.endEffect: Protective stop may fail on demand

rows.failureMode: Both interlock channels falsely indicate closed

notepad.html:
System boundary workshop

Purpose: Capture unresolved integration assumptions before they become controlled project records.

AssumptionOwnerEvidence needed
Forklift traffic is controlled during pallet exchangeProductionApproved traffic-management plan
All carton variants remain within the validated gripper envelopeMechanicalCarton specification and retention test

Review the Architecture and Engineering workflow before closing these assumptions.

brainstormRows.failureMode: Unexpected conveyor start during jam recovery
brainstormRows.effect: Hands or clothing can be drawn into conveyor pinch points while an operator clears a carton
brainstormRows.action: Define a deliberate hold-to-run recovery mode and inhibit robot automatic motion

phases.name: Define
phases.purpose: Agree intended use, boundaries, stakeholders, lifecycle, standards, and project responsibilities.

phases.name: Risk assessment
phases.purpose: Identify tasks, hazards, hazardous situations, and required risk reduction.

phases.name: Safety-oriented design
phases.purpose: Select architecture, safeguarding, safety functions, and testable requirements.

phases.name: Build and integrate
phases.purpose: Implement controlled hardware, software, configuration, and installation.

phases.name: Verify and validate
phases.purpose: Test safety functions, assumptions, interfaces, and production scenarios.

phases.name: Release and sustain
phases.purpose: Review residual risk, release evidence, train users, and control future changes.

activities.title: Define intended use and system boundary
activities.objective: Document product flow, robot tasks, human roles, interfaces, utilities, and lifecycle activities.
activities.inputs: User requirement specification, layout, production targets
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Have pallet exchange, jam recovery, teaching, maintenance, power restoration, and foreseeable misuse been included?
activities.standardReference: ISO 10218-2:2025 integration lifecycle; ISO 12100 risk assessment principles
activities.completionCriteria: Boundary, roles, interfaces, and lifecycle scope reviewed by controls, mechanical, production, and EHS representatives.
activities.evidence: System definition SD-01 and reviewed layout L-01

activities.title: Establish standards and verification strategy
activities.objective: Identify applicable standards, company rules, required assessments, and independent review needs.
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are standards applied to the complete robot application rather than only the robot arm?
activities.standardReference: ISO 10218-1:2025; ISO 10218-2:2025; ISO/TS 15066:2016 where collaborative operation is claimed
activities.completionCriteria: Standards register identifies scope, edition, responsibility, and planned evidence.

activities.title: Perform task-based hazard analysis
activities.objective: Analyze every operating task and lifecycle phase, not only automatic production.
activities.inputs: Layout, task list, incident history, supplier manuals
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Have people, access routes, stored energy, payloads, neighboring equipment, and restart conditions been considered?
activities.standardReference: ISO 12100 risk assessment and risk reduction
activities.completionCriteria: Hazards are linked to credible tasks and reviewed by people who perform those tasks.

activities.title: Determine required safety functions
activities.objective: Define functions needed to prevent or control hazardous motion and energy.
activities.outputs: Safety-function list and target integrity estimates
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Does each function define trigger, response, safe state, reset behavior, operating modes, and assumptions?
activities.standardReference: IEC 61508 lifecycle concepts and machinery-sector methods selected by the project
activities.completionCriteria: Safety functions have traceable hazards, target estimates, safe states, and validation concepts.

activities.title: Design safeguarding and access control
activities.objective: Define guards, access points, scanner fields, pallet opening, reset locations, and escape provisions.
activities.inputs: Risk assessment, stopping data, layout constraints
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Can a person reach or remain in the hazardous space without detection, and can reset occur with an obstructed view?
activities.standardReference: ISO 10218-2:2025 robot application and cell integration
activities.completionCriteria: Safeguarding distances and access controls are justified and reflected in the released layout.
activities.evidence: Open: scanner field and pallet-opening review required

activities.title: Analyze component and diagnostic failures
activities.objective: Identify failures that can defeat safety functions or create unsafe production behavior.
activities.inputs: Electrical design, safety architecture, supplier reliability data
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are common-cause failures, bypasses, diagnostic coverage, wiring faults, and loss of utilities addressed?
activities.standardReference: Project functional-safety plan and component data requirements
activities.completionCriteria: Critical failure modes have actions, owners, and traceable assumptions.
activities.evidence: FMEDA draft FM-01; supplier scanner data received

activities.title: Derive allocated safety requirements
activities.objective: Convert risk controls into measurable hardware, software, installation, and procedural requirements.
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Can every requirement be verified, and are timing, modes, reset, diagnostics, and fault response explicit?
activities.completionCriteria: Safety requirements link to hazards and identify verification methods.

activities.title: Implement controlled safety software and configuration
activities.objective: Configure safety PLC logic, scanner fields, robot safety parameters, and protected recipes under change control.
activities.outputs: Versioned safety application and configuration report
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are downloads, passwords, checksums, bypasses, and production recipe changes controlled and reviewable?
activities.standardReference: Company safety-software lifecycle and configuration-management procedure
activities.completionCriteria: Released versions are identified, reviewed, backed up, and reproducible.

activities.title: Commission interfaces and fault responses
activities.objective: Confirm wiring, I/O, network timeouts, pneumatic behavior, and restart logic before production trials.
activities.outputs: Commissioning records and corrected punch list
activities.safetyCheckpoint: What happens for broken wires, stale network data, sensor discrepancy, low pressure, power loss, and unexpected restart?
activities.completionCriteria: All safety I/O and defined fault reactions pass controlled commissioning checks.

activities.title: Validate safety functions under representative conditions
activities.objective: Test the integrated cell using realistic payload, speed, floor, access, fault, and recovery conditions.
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Do tests cover worst-case stopping, reset, muting, teaching, power restoration, and single-fault scenarios?
activities.standardReference: ISO 10218-2:2025 validation expectations and project validation plan
activities.completionCriteria: Every safety requirement has objective evidence or an approved deviation.

activities.title: Review residual risk and production readiness
activities.objective: Confirm unresolved issues, instructions, training, maintenance, and operational ownership before handover.
activities.inputs: Validation report, risk assessment, punch list, manuals
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Are open issues visible, authorized, time-bounded, and prevented from becoming undocumented production workarounds?
activities.completionCriteria: Authorized reviewers accept release evidence and residual risks; operators and maintenance personnel are trained.

activities.title: Control changes after release
activities.objective: Assess the safety impact of new cartons, pallet patterns, speeds, tools, software, and layout changes.
activities.inputs: Change request and current safety baseline
activities.safetyCheckpoint: Could the change invalidate stopping distance, reach prevention, payload retention, scanner fields, or prior evidence?
activities.completionCriteria: No safety-relevant change enters production without documented impact assessment and required revalidation.
activities.evidence: Open exercise: assess the heavy-carton recipe change

tests.title: Gate interlock and restart prevention
tests.objective: Validate protective stop and restart inhibition for gate access.
tests.method: Open the gate during automatic motion, challenge both channels, close the gate, and attempt restart without deliberate reset.
tests.expected: Hazardous motion stops and cannot restart until the gate is closed and reset is deliberately commanded.
tests.actual: Nominal gate opening passed; channel discrepancy test remains planned.

tests.title: Teach-mode speed and enabling-device validation
tests.objective: Confirm reduced-speed motion and enabling-device behavior inside the safeguarded space.
tests.method: Measure speed and challenge released, mid-position, and fully depressed enabling states.
tests.expected: Motion remains within the approved limit and stops for invalid enabling states.
tests.actual: All conditions passed within the approved limits.

evidence.title: Teach-mode validation report
evidence.description: Training example of configuration-specific validation evidence.

deviations.title: Person-sized access possible during pallet muting
deviations.disposition: Redesign muting geometry and repeat intrusion testing before production release.

changes.title: Heavy-carton production recipe
changes.reason: Increase carton mass, speed, and pallet height.
changes.safetyImpact: Payload retention, stopping performance, pallet stability, and protective-field assumptions require reassessment.
changes.verificationImpact: Repeat retention, stopping, path, and pallet-stability validation.

baselines.title: Training cell design baseline
baselines.scope: Architecture, safety requirements, safety configuration, and validation setup.

reviews.title: Cell concept safety review
reviews.scope: System boundary, tasks, hazards, and preliminary safety architecture.
reviews.decision: Proceed with actions for pallet opening and retained-energy controls.

interfaces.title: Scanner to safety PLC
interfaces.description: Protective-field, muting, and diagnostic state exchange.
interfaces.failureResponse: Invalid, stale, or discrepant state initiates protective stop and inhibits restart.

ram.title: Safety function diagnostic availability
ram.target: Safety functions available for at least 99.9% of scheduled production time
ram.method: Review event logs, bypass duration, and safety-related downtime monthly.

claims.title: Safeguarded access prevents exposure to hazardous robot motion
claims.argument: Interlocked access, protective fields, restart inhibition, and representative validation control access hazards.

fmea.failureMode: Person intrusion not detected at pallet opening
fmea.effect: Person can enter while robot remains capable of hazardous motion
fmea.action: Validate muting sequence, field geometry, sensor positioning, and fault response with person and pallet test pieces

fmea.failureMode: Gate opened but interlock reports closed
fmea.action: Use monitored dual-channel interlock and validate discrepancy detection and restart prevention

fmea.failureMode: Vacuum lost during high robot pose
fmea.effect: Carton drops or destabilizes pallet load
fmea.action: Add vacuum threshold monitoring, retained-energy assessment, and controlled placement strategy

fmea.failureMode: Automatic restart allowed after power restoration
fmea.effect: Robot or conveyor moves while personnel expect the cell to remain stopped
fmea.action: Require safety input validation and deliberate external reset after restoration

fmea.failureMode: Incorrect carton recipe selects unsafe path or placement
fmea.effect: Collision, dropped carton, or unstable pallet pattern
fmea.action: Protect recipe parameters, validate bounds, and require change-impact review

fmea.effect: Unexpected tooling movement or carton release during maintenance
fmea.action: Install lockable isolation and monitored dump; define zero-energy verification
